payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: prd_review.

Intent:
Make the supervisor produce agentic worker receipts by spawning workers through the built-but-unwired executor, running synchronously inline inside run_dual_agent_workflow. This is the first real graded fan-out path, not a detached job. When agentic_lead_policy is allowed or required, the supervisor plans and validates read-only workers, runs run_agentic_worker_fanout, captures supervisor-owned .handoff/agentic-workers receipts, and reuses existing P13/P14/evidence-grade verification without enabling fan-out by default or weakening the gate sequence.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: The PRD cleanly separates a new supervisor-owned producer from the existing P13/P14 verifier and forbids /lead self-stamping runtime_native (P3, ISS-3 AC)
Anti-goals match the intent: agentic mode stays default off; no eval harness, fourth reviewer, native team fan-out, or raw MCP reconnect
Allowed-policy non-blocking semantics are specified only as an ISS-1 acceptance criterion, not as a P-numbered public promise
P6 bounds per-agent timeout and budget but does not specify a maximum roster worker count for the inline synchronous fan-out
Artifact sha256 fidelity was not re-verified in-session because the hashing Bash commands were not approved

criteria: All planning artifacts present and readable at handoff-named paths
All 5 grill findings marked resolved with substantive resolutions
Producer/verifier boundary and read-only safety explicitly contracted
Anti-goals present and consistent with off-by-default intent
Per-slice acceptance criteria with named RED tests exist
evidence: accept: PRD promises, anti-goals, and per-slice acceptance criteria are complete enough to advance past prd_review
accept: producer/verifier split (P1-P3) and read-only enforcement (P2/P6, Finding 3) correctly preserve the quality boundary and keep fan-out off by default
revise-advisory: add an explicit promise contract for allowed-policy non-blocking semantics (currently only an ISS-1 acceptance criterion)
revise-advisory: extend P6 with an aggregate roster worker-count cap, not only per-agent timeout/budget, given synchronous inline fan-out
confidence.rationale: PRD content read directly from the handoff-named paths and judged against the stated intent; promises, anti-goals, and acceptance criteria are complete and the grill findings are all resolved, supporting accept. Confidence held below 0.9 because two genuine contract gaps remain (allowed-policy promise, aggregate roster cap) and artifact hashes could not be re-verified in-session.

payload.content: PRD for inline supervisor-owned agentic worker fan-out is well-scoped: clean producer/verifier split (lead plans roster, Codex spawns+hashes, P13/P14 verify), explicit read-only enforcement, gate-aware solo exception, and clear anti-goals keeping fan-out off-by-default. Accept to advance with two advisory refinements: add a promise contract for allowed-policy non-blocking semantics, and add an aggregate roster-cardinality bound for the inline synchronous fan-out (P6 only bounds per-agent).

assumptions_to_verify: that ISS-1's allowed-policy 'blocked production event, not success' is actually enforced at the public boundary (execution-gate concern, not prd_review)
that roster validation in implementation rejects an unbounded number of workers, not just over-budget/over-timeout per agent
that the working-tree implementation matches these promises (deferred to execution/outcome gate)
contradictions_checked: intent 'synchronously inline' vs PRD framing 'before the synthesis /lead call' - consistent
intent 'without enabling fan-out by default' vs Implementation Decisions 'agentic_lead_policy defaults to off' - consistent
intent 'reuses existing P13/P14' vs P3 'no second verifier, no self-stamped grades' - consistent
required-path forbidden outcomes vs allowed-path semantics - allowed path under-specified at promise level
missing_evidence: sha256 confirmation that the reviewed files match the handoff packet (hash commands not approved in-session)
an explicit PRD promise (not just an AC) for allowed-policy non-blocking behavior
a stated maximum worker-count bound for the inline fan-out
critical_review.strongest_objection: For a slice whose intent is synchronous inline fan-out, P6 bounds only per-agent timeout/budget and never caps total roster cardinality, so a large lead-planned roster could blow runtime/budget inside a single blocking gate call with no promised guardrail.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that the PRD already bounds aggregate roster size somewhere I missed, plus a promise-level statement of allowed-policy semantics, would move this to a clean high-confidence accept; conversely, if hash verification showed the reviewed PRD differs from the handoff packet, I would downgrade to revise/deny.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: issues_review.

Intent:
Make the supervisor produce agentic worker receipts by spawning workers through the built-but-unwired executor, running synchronously inline inside run_dual_agent_workflow. This is the first real graded fan-out path, not a detached job. When agentic_lead_policy is allowed or required, the supervisor plans and validates read-only workers, runs run_agentic_worker_fanout, captures supervisor-owned .handoff/agentic-workers receipts, and reuses existing P13/P14/evidence-grade verification without enabling fan-out by default or weakening the gate sequence.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: All 6 PRD promises are covered by ISS-1..ISS-5
Every PRD forbidden outcome maps to a concrete acceptance criterion
Executor and policy symbols referenced by issues.md exist in the working tree
Named test boundaries are real (driver/receipts/workers/executor files)
ISS-2/ISS-5 P6 timeout/budget ownership is explicitly disambiguated
Defaults remain off; P13/P14 reused without a duplicate verifier

criteria: Forbidden outcomes mapped to acceptance criteria (verified)
Named tests map to real public boundaries (grep-verified)
ISS-3 regression-claim code bodies not read (residual)
evidence: accept: issues.md is a complete and verifiable decomposition; advance the issues_review gate
confidence.rationale: For a planning-stage issues_review gate I directly verified PRD-promise coverage, forbidden-outcome-to-criteria mapping, symbol existence, and test-boundary grounding via grep, rather than trusting handoff claims. Residual uncertainty is that I did not read the ISS-3 implementation bodies to confirm the 'already exists and still passes' regression claim, and did not run the suite (out of scope for issues_review).

payload.content: issues.md decomposes the inline agentic-producer wiring into 5 slices (ISS-1..ISS-5) covering all six PRD promises with verifiable, boundary-grounded acceptance criteria. Source grep confirms the executor (run_agentic_worker_fanout, cleanup_orphaned_agentic_workers) and policy symbols exist, and named RED tests map to real public boundaries. Every PRD forbidden outcome maps to an acceptance criterion; read-only enforcement, opt-in default-off, P13/P14 reuse, and fail-closed negatives are preserved. Accept.

payload.content:
Independently review the tdd_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Make the supervisor produce agentic worker receipts by spawning workers through the built-but-unwired executor, running synchronously inline inside run_dual_agent_workflow. This is the first real graded fan-out path, not a detached job. When agentic_lead_policy is allowed or required, the supervisor plans and validates read-only workers, runs run_agentic_worker_fanout, captures supervisor-owned .handoff/agentic-workers receipts, and reuses existing P13/P14/evidence-grade verification without enabling fan-out by default or weakening the gate sequence.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Make the supervisor produce agentic worker receipts by spawning workers through the built-but-unwired executor, running synchronously inline inside run_dual_agent_workflow. This is the first real graded fan-out path, not a detached job. When agentic_lead_policy is allowed or required, the supervisor plans and validates read-only workers, runs run_agentic_worker_fanout, captures supervisor-owned .handoff/agentic-workers receipts, and reuses existing P13/P14/evidence-grade verification without enabling fan-out by default or weakening the gate sequence.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: The plan's design is implementable and partly already implemented in the uncommitted working tree.
Existing fail-closed required-policy protection is preserved because the producer only appends receipts and the unchanged verifier still blocks when required evidence is absent.
No agentic defaults are changed; agentic_lead_policy remains off by default per handoff.

criteria: Named symbols exist in source: yes
Producer threads into existing verifier, no duplicate: yes (stdio.py:654-661)
Full test suite green: unverified in-session
Plan file list complete: no (omits agentic_executor.py)
evidence: test_run_dual_agent_workflow_required_policy_still_blocks_without_executor_receipts (named in plan, not located in grep)
test_agentic_roster_validation_rejects_writable_or_missing_required_roles (named in plan, not located in grep)
test_agentic_worker_timeout_cleanup_runs_after_fanout_timeout (named in plan, not located in grep)
test_declared_runtime_native_from_non_supervisor_path_is_downgraded_and_blocked (named in plan, not located in grep)
test_submit_workflow_job_payload_round_trips_agentic_policy_fields (named in plan, not located in grep)
Plan correctly reuses existing verify_dynamic_workflow_receipts (P13/P14) rather than adding a second verifier.
Read-only worker contract enforced by pre-launch rejection is the correct, grill-aligned design.
Non-blocking revise recommendation: add supervisor/agentic_executor.py to the plan's file list and reconcile 'Add a producer function' wording with the already-built executor.
confidence.rationale: Core design (P13/P14 reuse, read-only enforcement, gate threading, allowed/required semantics) verified directly in source; all grill findings resolved; plan is decomposed and traceable. Reduced from higher because the plan's file list omits the central executor file and I could not execute the full suite in-session, and 5 named tests were not located by grep (acceptable for a plan gate but unverified).

payload.content: Implementation plan for inline supervisor-owned agentic worker production is sound, traceable, and grill-resolved. Verified against source: producer (produce_agentic_worker_receipts) is wired into run_dual_agent_workflow and threads receipts into the existing verify_dynamic_workflow_receipts (P13/P14) with no duplicate verifier; read-only enforced by rejection; gate identity threaded for solo-exception scoping. ACCEPT with one non-blocking objection: plan file list omits supervisor/agentic_executor.py.

assumptions_to_verify: Required policy with no executor receipts still blocks before synthesis at runtime
cleanup_orphaned_agentic_workers fires on timeout/dead-worker paths with supervisor-owned metadata
agentic defaults remain off across submit/poll payload conversion
contradictions_checked: Intent says 'built-but-unwired' but stdio.py:575-621 shows the producer IS wired in the working tree — resolved: implementation has run ahead of the plan (uncommitted), which is consistent with an implementation_plan gate written before/alongside execution
Plan step 4 'Add a producer function' vs producer already existing — flagged as a plan-accuracy nit, not a design defect
P3 'no second verifier' vs actual code — confirmed single verifier path
missing_evidence: Full `uv run --extra dev pytest -q` pass/fail output
Locations of 5 plan-named tests not found by grep (required-still-blocks, writable/missing-role rejection, timeout cleanup, non-supervisor runtime_native downgrade, submit payload round-trip)
Confirmation that .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/<worker>/ refs are actually written and hash-replay under P13 at runtime
critical_review.strongest_objection: The plan presents the producer/roster/validator as work to be added and omits supervisor/agentic_executor.py from its Files-To-Touch, even though that file already exists and is wired into run_dual_agent_workflow; a reviewer following the plan's file list would miss the slice's central file.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that the producer path can let required policy accept without supervisor-owned hash-replayable receipts (inverting fail-closed), or that producer receipts bypass verify_dynamic_workflow_receipts via a parallel evidence path — either would move this to DENY.

payload.content:
Independently review the implementation_plan gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Make the supervisor produce agentic worker receipts by spawning workers through the built-but-unwired executor, running synchronously inline inside run_dual_agent_workflow. This is the first real graded fan-out path, not a detached job. When agentic_lead_policy is allowed or required, the supervisor plans and validates read-only workers, runs run_agentic_worker_fanout, captures supervisor-owned .handoff/agentic-workers receipts, and reuses existing P13/P14/evidence-grade verification without enabling fan-out by default or weakening the gate sequence.

Corrective context from the previous round:
agents have not both accepted yet; revise and continue

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Make the supervisor produce agentic worker receipts by spawning workers through the built-but-unwired executor, running synchronously inline inside run_dual_agent_workflow. This is the first real graded fan-out path, not a detached job. When agentic_lead_policy is allowed or required, the supervisor plans and validates read-only workers, runs run_agentic_worker_fanout, captures supervisor-owned .handoff/agentic-workers receipts, and reuses existing P13/P14/evidence-grade verification without enabling fan-out by default or weakening the gate sequence.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Worker receipts land under .handoff/agentic-workers/<task>/<worker>/ (verified by ISS-1 test on-disk assertions)

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Make the supervisor produce agentic worker receipts by spawning workers through the built-but-unwired executor, running synchronously inline inside run_dual_agent_workflow. This is the first real graded fan-out path, not a detached job. When agentic_lead_policy is allowed or required, the supervisor plans and validates read-only workers, runs run_agentic_worker_fanout, captures supervisor-owned .handoff/agentic-workers receipts, and reuses existing P13/P14/evidence-grade verification without enabling fan-out by default or weakening the gate sequence.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Make the supervisor produce agentic worker receipts by spawning workers through the built-but-unwired executor, running synchronously inline inside run_dual_agent_workflow. This is the first real graded fan-out path, not a detached job. When agentic_lead_policy is allowed or required, the supervisor plans and validates read-only workers, runs run_agentic_worker_fanout, captures supervisor-owned .handoff/agentic-workers receipts, and reuses existing P13/P14/evidence-grade verification without enabling fan-out by default or weakening the gate sequence.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
agents have not both accepted yet; revise and continue

payload.content:
Independently review the outcome_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Make the supervisor produce agentic worker receipts by spawning workers through the built-but-unwired executor, running synchronously inline inside run_dual_agent_workflow. This is the first real graded fan-out path, not a detached job. When agentic_lead_policy is allowed or required, the supervisor plans and validates read-only workers, runs run_agentic_worker_fanout, captures supervisor-owned .handoff/agentic-workers receipts, and reuses existing P13/P14/evidence-grade verification without enabling fan-out by default or weakening the gate sequence.

Existing P13/P14/evidence-grade verification reused; gate sequence not weakened.
Read-only/bounds validation runs pre-launch and is test-covered.
Prior revise-cycle missing-test objections are resolved in current worktree.
confidence_criteria: produce_agentic_worker_receipts wired inline before P13 with not_applicable default
All source/tdd.md named tests exist and pass independently
586-test full suite green in independent Cursor session
Final outcome_review event accepted with P1-P4 green
On-disk live-smoke worker artifacts with status passed
outcome.confidence_rationale: High confidence: source wiring, off-by-default invariant, and P13 ordering verified directly; all TDD-named tests present and independently green (586 full suite); final outcome_review accepted with all probes green. Held below 0.95 because live-smoke pass lacks an archived rerun result JSON in docs (only on-disk worker refs and test-evidence.md), and agentic_executor.py remains untracked in git.

assumptions_to_verify: On-disk .handoff/agentic-workers/agentic-executor-live-smoke-20260531/audit-1/ artifacts correspond to the rerun cited in test-evidence.md
No concurrent outcome_review holds the handoff lock (lock file absent at review time)
contradictions_checked: Claude claim gate never graded: superseded—outcome-review event 416618 shows accepted with P1-P4 green
Earlier revise missing-test claims: disproved—all TDD-named tests now exist and pass
Default-on risk: disproved—policy off returns not_applicable
Bundled live-smoke-result.json blocked vs receipt passed: reconciled as stale first-run artifact; on-disk worker refs show successful rerun
missing_evidence: agentic-live-smoke-result-rerun1.json not present under docs/dual-agent/agentic-executor-wiring-inline-20260531/
agentic_executor.py and tests/test_agentic_executor.py untracked (??) in git status
End-to-end real Claude subprocess fan-out not exercised in CI (live smoke is operator-local)
critical_review.strongest_objection: Live-smoke pass is not fully replay-archived: the wiring task folder contains a blocked agentic-live-smoke-result.json while the receipt cites a successful rerun1 JSON that is absent from docs/, weakening audit trail for the first real graded fan-out path.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If replay-verifying the on-disk live-smoke worker refs fails hash/ref integrity, if a fresh required-policy integration run without fake runners fails to produce runtime_native P13 acceptance, or if full pytest regresses on a clean checkout.

decisions: accept
objections: Bundled agentic-live-smoke-result.json in the wiring task folder records a blocked pre-wiring run; the passed rerun JSON (rerun1) cited in receipts is not archived under docs/, so live-smoke pass relies on test-evidence.md plus on-disk worker refs rather than a replay-exported result artifact.
outcome.summary: Inline agentic-worker fan-out wiring meets PRD P1–P6: produce_agentic_worker_receipts is off-by-default, runs synchronously before P13 in run_dual_agent_workflow, validates read-only/bounded rosters pre-launch, appends supervisor-owned .handoff/agentic-workers receipts, and reuses existing P13/P14 verification. Earlier revise objections (missing TDD tests) are resolved—all plan-named tests exist and pass. Independent verification: 28 focused + 586 full pytest green. Final outcome_review accepted (event 416618, all probes green). Live smoke corroborated by on-disk worker artifacts; archived rerun JSON missing from docs.
